remarks.text: Harness Engineering for Self-Improvement
remarks.content: The concept of recursive self-improvement (RSI) dates back to I. J. Good (1965), where he defined an “ultraintelligent machine” as a system that can surpass humans in all intellectual activities and design better machines to improve itself. Yudkowsky (2008) used the phrase “recursi
remarks.contentTranslation: 递归自我提升（ RSI ）的概念可以追溯到I. J. Good （ 1965 ） ，他将“超智能机器”定义为能够在所有智力活动中超越人类并设计更好的机器来提升自身的系统。Yudkowsky （ 2008 ）使用了短语“recursi
remarks.translation: 自我提升的线束工程

remarks.text: Six AI agent SDKs for enterprise Kubernetes, compared
remarks.content: There’s a question we hear constantly from platform and engineering leaders right now, “which agent SDK should we standardize on for our Kubernetes clusters?” The honest answer is that the question is slightly wrong, and... The post Six AI agent SDKs for enterprise Kubernetes, comp
remarks.contentTranslation: 我们现在经常从平台和工程领导那里听到一个问题， “我们应该为我们的Kubernetes集群标准化哪个AgentSDK ？“老实说，问题有点不对劲，而且……企业Kubernetes发布的Six AI agent SDK ，
remarks.translation: 六个用于企业Kubernetes的AIAgentSDK ，比较

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
imo this is the most impt part of anthropic's J-space paper today. it's a two-parter:

1) ant proved that they can do "brain surgery" interventions into reasoning to change topics midstream*
2) THE MODEL IS ABLE TO DETECT WHAT INTERVENTION WAS DONE - close cousin to eval awareness**

*control > correlation - this convincingly demonstrates understanding

**this was prompted awareness... surely @mlpowered's team also tried to eval unprompted awareness but i didn't see evidence of that
remarks.translation:
国际海事组织这是当今Anthropic J-space论文中最直接的部分。这是两个参数： 1 ） ANT证明他们可以在推理过程中进行“脑外科手术”干预，以改变主题* 2 ）模型能够检测到所做的干预-与评估意识* * *控制>相关性密切相关-这令人信服地表明理解* *这是提示的意识...
当然， @ mlpowered的团队也试图评估未经提示的意识，但我没有看到证据
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.translation: @ aiDotEngineer @ cursor_ai此外，不要受标题/角色的限制。尽管我不是受过培训的工程师/设计师，但我发现Cursor/AI Engineer/Config的演讲非常有帮助。现在每个人都是工程师、项目经理和设计师

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
If you wanna level up as an AI builder, binge watch the talks from these 3 conferences (all freshly wrapped). You can literally learn from the best in the field from the comfort of your home.

- AI Engineer @aiDotEngineer https://t.co/zsLvuWWBMl
- Cursor Compile @cursor_ai https://t.co/EzpGKd8O4Y
- Figma Config https://t.co/m3KdubDk0i

It's crazy that all of this high-quality knowledge is freely shared on the Internet (the tickets to these conferences often cost hundreds of dollars), and I'm surprised that most of these videos only get tens of thousands of views. High-quality conference talks are massively, massively underrated! 

Watching on YouTube is also arguably a better experience than attending in person: no bad audio, no crowd blocking the screen, no distractions, and you can pause to take notes
remarks.translation:
如果您想升级为AI构建者，请观看这3场会议的演讲（全部刚刚结束）。您可以在舒适的家中向现场最优秀的人学习。
- AI Engineer @ aiDotEngineer - Cursor Compile @ cursor_ai - Figma Config在互联网上免费分享所有这些高质量的知识真是太疯狂了（这些会议的门票通常要花费数百美元） ，我很惊讶这些视频中的大多数只能获得数万次观看。高质量的会议演讲被大大低估了！
在YouTube上观看也可以说是比亲自出席更好的体验：没有糟糕的音频，没有人群阻挡屏幕，没有分心，您可以停下来做笔记
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
🆒 eve evals itself with 𝚎𝚟𝚎 𝚎𝚟𝚊𝚕.

Web frameworks made testing an ecosystem choice. e.g.: React didn’t ship with a built-in testing solution.

For agents, evals are essential… thus we ship 𝚎𝚟𝚎 𝚎𝚟𝚊𝚕 out of the box. For your agents and eve’s own evolution. https://t.co/Ujh4FHpPdN
remarks.translation: 🆒 夏娃用“”来逃避自己。Web框架使测试成为一种生态系统选择。例如： React没有内置测试解决方案。对于Agent商来说，淘汰是必不可少的……因此，我们开箱即用。为您的Agent和Eve自己的进化。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
This is how media is a bludgeon for an entrenched class of doom loop politicians

They want their doom loop politician Gary McCoy in at any cost and will lie, cheat and steal to do it

And legacy media does nothing to stop it

Pure sociopathy
remarks.translation: 这就是媒体如何成为根深蒂固的厄运循环政治家的棍棒他们希望他们的厄运循环政治家加里·麦考伊不惜一切代价，并且会撒谎、欺骗和偷窃。传统媒体没有做任何事情来阻止它纯粹的社会病态

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
The ultimate test for coding AI: is software as a whole getting better? Are companies shipping faster? Do you now have apps and games you couldn't even dream of before? Is the software you're getting bug-free?

For me: my own ability to influence my productivity has radically increased. I feel a level of agency and autonomy I never had before. I'm free to create and execute. I can communicate a vision with my colleagues instantly. Personal software is real and a big win, I have a bunch of web tools and macOS apps I wrote just for myself.

For Vercel: it's been amazing to see our CTO @cramforce bring novel software to life with swaths of agents. Genuinely useful software that's having an impact industry-wide emerged nearly autonomously (https://t.co/zwHPbMHK45, https://t.co/TTbSu13EjV, https://t.co/HedSpZpVQI). That's just an example. Our PM team ships software and fixes bugs. Our junior engineers and interns ship quality software at scale. On a personal note, AI at my fingertips, as CLI or v0, allows me to keep up with all the product updates. I can build demos, apps and benchmarks on top of @vercel as new products go out.

But the ultimate jury is that people should love our products, expand their usage, refer their friends and colleagues to the platform. So far that tracks well, and it's the main metric I watch (and obviously we take token costs seriously, just like any other infrastructure spend… there isn't and has never been a proclamation to "tokenmaxxx").
remarks.translation:
AI编码的终极考验：软件作为一个整体是否正在变得更好？公司的运输速度是否更快？您现在有以前无法想象的应用程序和游戏吗？您获得的软件是否无错误？对我来说：我自己影响生产力的能力大大提高了。我感受到了前所未有的能动性和自主性。我可以自由创建和执行。我可以立即与同事沟通愿景。
个人软件是真实的，是一个巨大的胜利，我有一堆我为自己编写的网络工具和macOS应用程序。对于Vercel ：很高兴看到我们的CTO @ cramforce通过大量Agent将新颖的软件带入生活。对整个行业产生影响的真正有用的软件几乎是自主出现的（这只是一个例子。我们的PM团队提供软件并修复错误。我们的初级工程师和实习生大规模提供高质量的软件。
就个人而言，指尖上的AI，如CLI或v0 ，使我能够跟上所有产品更新的步伐。随着新产品的推出，我可以在@ vercel之上构建演示、应用和基准测试。但最终的评审团是，人们应该喜欢我们的产品，扩大他们的使用范围，将他们的朋友和同事推荐到平台。
到目前为止，跟踪良好，这是我观察的主要指标（显然我们认真对待代币成本，就像任何其他基础设施支出一样......没有也没有宣布过“tokenmaxxx” ）。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
Fable 5 will leave Claude subscriptions tomorrow at midnight. Here are 5 use cases worth trying before then, with prompts you can copy and paste:

1) FIND FABLE-WORTHY WORK
You're Fable 5, the most capable model available. Look through my projects, docs, and your memory, and list the top 5 tasks genuinely worth running on you. Rank them with a one-line reason each. Don't do the work yet.

2) GET LIFE & BUSINESS ADVICE
You're my business advisor. Read my plan doc and pull live data from my connected tools. Write a one-page assessment of my business and the top 3 things to focus on for the next 3 months, plus what to drop and why.

3) MAKE YOUR PROJECT SHIP-READY
I'm about to ship this project. Find everything wrong with it first. Read the whole codebase and hunt for real bugs, broken edge cases, and anything that'll break in front of a user. List each issue with how to reproduce it and the fix. Hold a high bar.

4) PLAN THE NEXT BIG THING
I want to plan a big project: [describe it]. Don't build yet. Lay out the full plan: phases, key decisions, risks, and open questions. Flag anything that could sink it, and make it clear enough that a cheaper model could execute step by step.

5) REFACTOR YOUR AI SKILLS
Refactor my AI skill system. Read all my skills, map where they overlap or conflict, then clean them up: tighten workflows, cut bloat, and standardize structure. Keep each skill working and check in at major milestones.

📌 Watch my tutorial for a live demo of all 5 use cases: https://t.co/XElMEV3FwK
remarks.translation:
寓言5将于明天午夜离开Claude订阅。以下是之前值得一试的5个用例，并提示您可以复制和粘贴： 1)查找功能强大的作品您是Fable 5 ，这是功能最强大的模型。浏览我的项目、文档和您的记忆，并列出真正值得您完成的前5项任务。对他们进行排名，并各列出一条理由。暂时不要做这项工作。2)获取生活和商业建议您是我的商业顾问。
阅读我的计划文档，并从连接的工具中提取实时数据。写一页评估我的业务和未来3个月要关注的前3件事，以及要放弃的内容和原因。3 ）让您的项目发货，我即将发货。先找出问题所在。阅读整个代码库，寻找真正的漏洞、边缘故障以及用户面前的任何故障。列出每个问题，以及如何重现和修复。
保持高标准。4 ）计划我想计划一个大项目的下一件大事： [描述它]。暂时不要建造。制定完整的计划：阶段、关键决策、风险和未决问题。标记任何可能使其沉没的东西，并明确说明更便宜的模型可以逐步执行。5 ）重构您的AI技能重构我的AI技能系统。
阅读我的所有技能，绘制它们的重叠或冲突地图，然后清理它们：收紧工作流，减少膨胀，并使结构标准化。保持每项技能发挥作用，并在重要里程碑时进行检查。📌观看我的教程，了解所有5个用例的实时演示：
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Great post on how to think about open source AI and the applied AI layer. Two things will always be true in AI. Frontier intelligence will likely remain at the forefront of solving brand new used cases and often be used for orchestrating and planning of any type of complex workflow. 

At the same time, as use-cases become mature and predictable in an enterprise context, you can begin to peel off some set of the tokens to either lower cost open or closed models *or* models that are trained for the task at hand. 

Doing this too early in the adoption curve of any new use-case doesn’t make sense as you don’t know what you’re optimizing for, which is why there will always be a bit of a lag here.

This process can essentially run on forever as there is no end for both the benefits of frontier intelligence or tuned models. This is why the spend and token volume for both approaches will continue to go up for years to come as we’re still early on both.

Again, the only reason this dynamic is possible though is because of the applied AI layer that can effectively eval their workflows in a specific domain, choose a mixture of models to solve tasks, and have enough scale to eventually train their own models for their purposes.
remarks.translation:
关于如何思考开源AI和应用AI层的精彩文章。在AI中，有两件事永远是正确的。Frontier Intelligence可能会继续处于解决全新用例的最前沿，并经常用于协调和规划任何类型的复杂工作流。
与此同时，随着用例在企业环境中变得成熟和可预测，您可以开始剥离一些令牌集，以获得为手头任务训练的低成本开放或封闭模型*或*模型。在任何新用例的采用曲线中过早这样做是没有意义的，因为你不知道你在优化什么，这就是为什么这里总是会有一些滞后。
这个过程基本上可以永远运行，因为前沿智能或优化模型的好处是没有尽头的。这就是为什么两种方法的支出和代币数量将在未来几年继续上升，因为我们在这两种方法上都还处于早期阶段。
同样，这种动态可能的唯一原因是因为所应用的AI层可以有效地评估特定领域的工作流，选择混合模型来解决任务，并且具有足够的规模，最终可以为自己的目的训练自己的模型。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
VCs: this job is investing in outliers. 

Also VC: here’s our revolutionary sourcing algorithm, custom built by our in house team, RLed on decades of data, with proprietary data signals from the moment someone is born. 

This job is very weird where there is so much serendipity and luck involved in how you stumbled upon an investment. However, it does take a lot of conviction and if you have years of bets where you have been right, then you aren’t simply “lucky”. 

Returns are the only alpha.. but those take time and so we tend to resort to random metrics to prove how we’re different from the next VC. 

The unscientific way I have tried to play this game is by massively expanding my surface of luck to try to find these outliers generally and having a prepared mind for 3-4 areas that are 6-12 months early where I can meet every entrepreneur. 

Time will only tell if this works!
remarks.translation:
风险投资：这项工作是投资异常值。还有VC ：这是我们的革命性采购算法，由我们的内部团队定制，基于数十年的数据进行RL ，并从某人出生的那一刻开始使用专有数据信号。这份工作非常奇怪，因为你如何偶然发现投资涉及如此多的偶然性和运气。然而，这确实需要很多信念，如果你有多年的赌注，你是对的，那么你不仅仅是“幸运的”。
回报是唯一的Alpha ，但这需要时间，因此我们倾向于使用随机指标来证明我们与下一个VC的不同之处。我尝试玩这个游戏的不科学的方法是大规模地扩展我的运气表面，试图找到这些异常值，并为提前6-12个月的3-4个领域做好准备，在那里我可以遇到每个企业家。时间只会证明这是否有效！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Nan Yu
remarks.role: head of product linear
remarks.text:
The biggest advantage that 20-year-old me had over today me is the ability to slam unbelievable hours into work. 

But looking back, those hours were mostly spent on tedious programming tasks, which are largely automated now. 

996 still has uses, but it’s not as universally effective as it was.
remarks.translation: 20岁的我今天拥有的最大优势是能够将难以置信的工作时间投入到工作中。但回顾过去，这些时间主要花在繁琐的编程任务上，这些任务现在基本上是自动化的。996仍然有用，但它并不像以前那样普遍有效。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。
